title: 30 Minute Meeting with Chris  (Google meet) - Leo and Chris
meeting_title: 30 Minute Meeting with Chris  (Google meet) - Leo and Chris

transcript.text: Yeah, no problem.
Okay.
Can you hear me?
Yeah, I can hear you fine, yeah.
I can hear you fine, if you can hear me.
Yeah, awesome.
Long time to see you.
How have you been doing?
Yeah, how's business?
Yeah, from my side, all good.
How's yours?
Yeah, yeah, we got really deep into the AI stuff, and I'm certainly finding it an enjoyable period of work.
But just with, you know, what you can do.
Which are the models you're using mostly?
Claude Code still, or are you using Codex, or?
Yeah, so now I'm doing Claude Code, because it's like a lot of new applications, but I think it's basically like the same.
It's just like wrapping different software.
But now I'm trying to make...
Have you tried OpenClaw?
Yeah, I tried, but it's okay.
But for the stuff I'm doing now, so it's not so useful.
Yeah.
Yeah, nice.
Tell me some use cases if you think what you could help me with.
What sort of things do you think would be the best spend of your time?
Mm-hmm.
It depends on your priorities, but for the last couple of months, I'm more focusing now for things more tied to sales, business development, like this stuff.
And I'm coding to support it, like dashboards or sales automations or like in this area.
So if you have anything in mind that's like from this area, it would be good.
Yeah.
Well, I...
I'm always wanting to recruit more owners on the national park, property owners that rent out their properties.
And the workflow is generally, I try and book a call with me.
I find that's the most useful step if I can bring a personality, like a person to the sign-up process.
That's normally quite helpful.
Then I'm generally quite successful at getting them to sign up.
One of the places, it's funny, I was thinking about this last night, is a lot of owners are on Airbnb.
But Airbnb is quite clever about restricting the contact details for the owners.
And, you know, it's funny, I was thinking about this last night.
I mean, it's like, do you use Playwright or any of those kind of, you know, like it will control?
Or the browser, like, because often they'll write into the description, the name of the property, and then you can do a web search to go in, like, so you can find.
so basically, there is a lot of scrapers, you can find in Appify, and I'm sure it's like plenty of like Airbnb.
So for your case, I'm just start, like, researching.
So maybe some of them can be LinkedIn, for example, if they own, they may be property investors in, like, UK.
I think my gut instinct is that they're typically 60 years old, not very tech-savvy, you know, like an email is about as far as they'll get within the technology world.
So I don't think you'll find them much on LinkedIn, but, yeah, the ultimate is trying to find an email address, even once we've got the email.
We can automate our emails pretty easily with the API, but collecting email addresses and then maybe even the physical address, because we could post something out to their letterbox.
That's another thing, Yeah, yeah.
I think if, like, market is very small, like yours, it's better do whatever it takes to reach out to them.
So it can, first, emails, then get calls to set an appointment, and then if nothing works, like, send a letter.
So, like that, because, yeah, if market's big, like, I don't know, recruitment companies, U.S.
or others, you can just blast thousands of emails every day.
But if it's more small, it takes more time to find these emails.
So sometimes...
sometimes...

It can be, I don't know, some like scrapers, as you say, from some sources that's not so easy to find, or maybe some databases, maybe there is like database of all owners, or maybe some, I don't know, website and internet, some forum where they're discussing.

So, you know, some non-obvious places where they can be, so yeah, in these cases, I'm just asking like CGPT or plot, okay, if this is our like market, what are best places to find it, and then just find it.
So when find it, just make a campaign, like write good emails, and then send, and if it doesn't work, you can send it next month and next month until it's worked.
So, yeah, I mean, I'm happy for you to spend some hours on that, if you want, like, you did a good job last time.
I feel like, yeah, that kind of outreach, that kind of data collection of the contact details is, you know, it'd be a good one.

I'm sure there's other things you could do as well, but ultimately my kind of North Star is creating sales from the properties I've got and like, you know, finding new inventory, which are the properties to kind of sign up with us.
And, yeah, you know, why don't we agree that, you know, a certain amount of money or something, you can see what you can come up with.
You certainly added some good value last time.
I particularly liked the change log.
Do you remember you added that on the, I think, when we were doing the GitHub repositories?
It was, it was, it that was an improvement of our workflow when we were, you know, working with Claude Code.
It's keeping a record with.
With everything we've done.
Yeah, yeah, yeah.

So, yeah, there's sort of, yeah, there's little moments of things that can be really useful, and, yeah, certainly I've got confidence in your ability using the AI, and if there's value that you can add, I'm happy to, you know, create another project for us.
Yeah, it's great.
So, what I'm thinking, if you have some special ideas outside of what we're talking, I'm open to hear, like, what's your ideas.
But last time we discussed that you have, like, big database of emails already, like, the customers or, like, prospects you contacted.
So, have you done anything with this, or it's still in the list?
Yeah, we've got, we've got, yeah, we've got some owners that have maybe been in touch with us, and then, no.
We've never converted them to sign up.
So there's a kind of list along those lines.
We've tried various things.
Like I tried like a MailChimp campaign where it would send, you know, every few weeks, like some kind of follow-up to them.
Yeah, some of them are just, they just get a bit stuck at that crossing the line of committing to it.
I've definitely found an improvement in my workflow when I actually try and talk to them, like brings a bit of a face to the business.
So that's what I asked my team to do.
If anyone is a prospecting client, just book a meeting with me and I talk to them for 30 minutes.
And I feel like that's as good as I can do.
And then we follow them up with emails.
But I also think there's a whole set of people that have maybe even never heard of us.
Let me share my screen just to kind of show you.
Okay, let's say I pick a destination, which is, I think you can go, so, actually this map isn't the best here, let me just show you.
Yeah, so, look, we're in the UK here, this is like the UK, and this green area is the national park that we cover.
So it's very geographically targeted, and actually one of the, this is, this destination here is where.
We've got the strongest amount of properties and inventory.

That's like where I started the business, but there's another area down here, which is called Linton, and this is full, it's actually called Linmouth and Linton, and this is full of holiday properties.

So if I search this area here, Linton, if they're coming on Airbnb, you can start to see there's like 200 places to stay here, which is quite a lot, but I reckon a lot of these have never even heard of us.
It's like, if I take this one, for example, in the description here, they've got Lin Cottage.
Sometimes you can find a website or something.
And in website, they have phone?
Yeah.
So here you go.
So they've got a website and then this is the data that I want.
And for this one, once we've got that spreadsheet, we'll be able to see whether we've had any contact with them.
You see this one, we've never contacted.
So that would be an example of a good person to contact.
And often the description there.
So I don't know why they're running some sort of scraper tool that can take these descriptions out and then search online to find if they've got a website.
So this is exactly kind of target audience where, yeah, we don't tend to do like a room in a house.
We tend to do like a whole property where they've got a kitchen.
So if they've got a kitchen, that is.
That's signal.
That's our audience.
But this person may have never heard of our business and we're happy to advertise alongside of them in, you know, like they can keep their existing listing.
We just want to be like dual market with them.
So that's, you know, that's, that's, I feel like that could be useful.
Like, yeah, yeah, I think we're about like 500 or something that has never heard of us.

And if we can convert some of those with email address and like postal number or phone number, like we can, we can then, you know, once we get to that point, we could ask your advice as well, what you think would help convert, but ultimately email outreach and maybe postal something in the post and get them to book a call with me.
Or I think just call them, then say, this is like what you're doing.
Interested, and if they're interested, they can call you, and you can walk them through and present them in service.
Yeah.
Yeah.
How do you want to do it?
I can't remember what we did payment-wise last time.
I think we just agreed an amount, didn't we?
And I paid you up front or something.
I can't remember.
You didn't like the hourly rate, which is fine.
Yeah.
So I think if it's more like, you know, sales, business development thing, that's what I'm focusing on.
We can make like some fix, like, I don't know, collecting data.
This is like technical stuff, like scraper, to find all the contacts.
And also, if you want, I can reach out to them on my sites via email, or I don't know, if you use CRM, I can make some process, like new CRM, some campaign.
So you can monitor it.
So I can also, like call them.
So I'll make a process that's then.
Yeah, can convert into meetings because it's like small markets and I think it's better not only sending emails but also calling and whatever needs to close.
So if we can make it, so it can be like process, repeatable, maybe for this area when we've done it, so you will have like a process that you can do like for other areas.
So I don't know if you have plans to extend in like, because you have kind of like software and...
it's, it's, yeah, that's, that's not a short answer that for me.

So in terms of like the front, the front end, like business that competes with Airbnb, I've ended up with just this, this one national park, which has this, you know, it's like, we have our own Airbnb kind of style search here.
I have, I don't...
I plans, I think, to expand this part of the business outside of this area.
But what I am building is the property management system, which is this part of it, which collects the bookings from these channels.
They come from Airbnb, Booking.com, and our local website.
This is a scalable business that then you could plug this into another part of the world.
So let's say, don't know, aren't you still in Bali?
Are in Bali?
Yeah, still.
So let's say someone in Bali wanted to recreate this business in Bali.
They need the property management software, which is this part, which I think is a part that I would rather sell and help people scale.
So that's the bit that I'm trying to work on, is people who want to grow an agency in a pocket of the world, you know, to get, you know, them.
To give them the software to do that.
And that's what BookingBrain, that's what the software does.
You know, you have to kind of onboard your properties and then you can manage a certain area.

So, so yeah, I don't really want to, I don't really want to scale the, I don't really want to take on Airbnb, but I feel like there's a good business if you just pick a, pick a, pick a geographical area and you just become like a local business.
Yeah.
That works alongside an Airbnb.
Hmm.
Yeah, I think it's, uh, can be done because a lot of places in the world, so it's can more easily to reach out to these people.
So, but as you say, it's like, you're thinking about it.
So it's, can we do like, I don't test campaign to see what's like reaction and so on.
But I think, uh, first we can start from.
Um, .
These areas in England, so, and then see how it goes, and later, if you will decide that it's a priority, we can make some tests, campaign, outreach for your book and brain.
What about, how much do you want to be paid for the, like, how do you want to separate the, do you want to do the outreach initially, just for that part?

I mean, I could imagine a model that I can give you, like, an incentive of a commission for each sign-up, or something like that, like, you know, that, I can imagine a model where we do that, but initially, I just want to pay you for the time, or, you know, whatever, for the, for the kind of outreach, and then once we've got the database, we need to kind of, like, figure out how best to convert them.
Yeah, I think if creating the workflow that can,

Like scrape, or I think if it's just one-time job, because if you have these properties, you will not do this again, I think, or maybe, I don't know, every year you can do it again, maybe some new owners will have something.
So we also can make like a workflow.
So when I scrape it on my site, I can give it like to you, so you can use it later if you need.
So this is one aspect, like building a list, like a pipeline of owners in this park for who we can reach out.
So once this is done, I think, yeah, it's better like fix price, because it's very, you know, straightforward what we need to do.
And then for, if I help you to reach out to them in different ways, like make a kind of success, system, when we start in one chat.
Yeah, can do like commission base.
So, so kind of when you have a meeting with them, you qualify it, you pay me some commission.
So, yeah, I mean, I'm essentially for incentive models, you know, to help me grow.
Yeah, I've got one side of sales, which is growing the inventory or the properties.
And then I've got another side of sales, which is getting people to book the properties.
So there's two, there's two channels of sales really.
But yeah, ultimately, I feel like there's no reason why I couldn't have, you know, 90% of the inventory on the national park.

Like there's no reason for not to sign up with me because they can also advertise with Airbnb, you know, it's like, it's a way of just giving their property more exposure on different marketing channels.
So, you know, there's no, there's no,
There's reason why they wouldn't be able to sign all of them up because they can continue their other marketing elsewhere.
And that's what the channel manager does quite well is it connects the calendars to stop double bookings via the API.
Yeah, I think if you have CRM, we can make it in your CRM, so it's more easy to connect with any systems you have, databases.
So like with CRM, then like it checks if this client is in your database, have you contacted and so on.
So kind of make a process, sales process that we will use later to help them.
That sounds good.
How much can I pay you?
So we can start just from, I don't know, like milestones.
So first we need to collect this data.
So we need to scrape this then.
is at need need to prepare for this
To show me, find the websites, scrape the phone numbers, or like anything we have.
So this is kind of one milestone I see.
Then it's, when it's done, like, so also we should validate it from your database, but I think better do it in your CRM.
So once we find it, we edit a new CRM, and from there, we go further.
So for this scraping, I need to check, but I think like three, four hundred dollars to create their database that we will use further.
And, yeah, then we see, so what CRM are you using?
We're using Freshdesk.
It's got, it's got a very good API.
So it's...

We've got good integration with Claude, so yeah, if we can get the email addresses and any data that we've got, we can query the database, like you said, with checking past communication, and we'll be able to personalize emails outbound based on what we know about their business.
So, yeah, that part has been significantly improved with Claude Code helping on those types of things.
Good.
So, once we have all this database, I think, then we integrate it in CRM.
So, for example, if we have, like, fonts or anything, then we upload it in your CRM, then we validate it through your, like, database API or everything.
So, like this.
Yeah, that's cool.
Yeah, Well, I'm happy with that.
Yeah, The...

The, yeah, things to remember is target properties that have a kitchen so people can cook within the property because sometimes if it's just a room with no kitchen, that's not really our target market.
And then also within the National Park of Exmoor, but we do do the surrounding areas.
So if they're 20 miles outside or something, 20 kilometers, that's fine.
They tend to be happy with that.
So it's not like a strict boundary.
It's within the region of the National Park.
And then I would say the biggest area for growth in terms of population is probably Linton, which is that one I showed you, spelled L-Y-N-T-O-N.
And below that one, there's Linmouth.
It's a bit smaller, but they're often like a big brother and a little brother.
They're considered together.
People talk about Linton and Linmouth.
And yeah.

I think just scraping as much information as we can into a spreadsheet will give us a really good milestone to try and convert those, you know, and if you can help me, if you can help me sign up new owners, I could give you some incentive, like, I don't know, when we take the first, like the first booking of the new property is when I actually make some money out of it, because I don't make anything until, we take the first booking, but at the point of the first booking, I could, you know, I could give you a commission on that sale, basically, as they're trying to try and incentivize you, then, you know, once we've done that project, you might be able to then help me just generate more sales, like as well, I've got, I've got this website.
Yeah, yeah, I think it's really what I like to do.
So if you will need help, I will definitely help you.
There's various avenues there.
There's social media, marketing, there's Google AdWords.
Yeah.
So I want to kind of ask, so if you have, if you sign a new property, like what's your goals for, I don't know, 2026?
How many new properties you want to sign?
Have you any specific numbers in your mind?
Yeah.
I I, we, we've got about 250 properties and I reckon there's about a thousand, like our target would be to get to about a thousand.
So I feel like we can times our business by about four, four times.
But it's a bit slow onboarding people.
I could, you know, if we did, if we did suddenly onboard a lot of people, we could, we could take on more people to help with that.
Yeah.
I've got another call in four minutes and I need to do a little bit of preparation for it.
Okay.
Yeah.
So let's start with what we've discussed.
I'm also happy to pay you in advance.
I know I've worked with you before, so I trust you.
And yeah, the end goal would be to collate a big spreadsheet of 250, 500, something like that, of people that we could start an outreach campaign for.
Okay, awesome.
I will recap this call and do some research.
I will send you in WhatsApp.
So yeah, it's good.
I have like PayPal, if you're okay.
I might convert you to email, any, because I find email a bit easier with dealing with things on volume.
But if you use the email address, stay at the best of Exmoor.co.uk.
I'm sure you would have sent it there last time.
Okay, okay.
Just sharing with me your email.
will...
because WhatsApp, I definitely get a bit behind you, because I tend to use it for personal, and then I have like 25 in red messages.
So send a email.
I'll be better at responding there.
Okay, cool.
So...
So...
I want to start, like, this week, or what's your timeline?
Whatever you you want, yeah.
Just send me an invoice with the milestones and I'll pay you in advance.
Okay, Chris, nice talking to you.
We'll reach out to you later.
Cheers, mate.
Yeah, bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Define a project to acquire new property owners.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=119.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Project:** Acquire new properties for "Best of Exmoor" by scraping Airbnb for owner contact info (email, phone, address) to build a sales pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=534.0)
  - [**Goal:** Grow inventory from \~250 to 1,000 properties, quadrupling the business.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1507.0)
  - [**Scope:** Focus on the Exmoor National Park region, prioritizing the Linton/Lynmouth area, and target properties with a kitchen.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
  - [**Phased Payment:** A fixed fee (\~$300–$400) for data collection, followed by a commission on new owner sign-ups.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1245.0)

## Topics

### Problem: Slow Owner Acquisition

  - [**Goal:** Grow inventory from \~250 to 1,000 properties.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1507.0)
  - [**Challenge:** Airbnb restricts owner contact details, making direct outreach difficult.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=151.0)
  - [**Current Process:** Book a 30-min call with Chris → high conversion rate.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=506.0)
  - [**Target Profile:** Non-tech-savvy owners (\~60+ years old) for whom email is the primary digital channel.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=216.0)

### Solution: Data Scraping & Outreach

  - [**Strategy:** Scrape Airbnb listings for property names, then web-search to find owner contact info (website, email, phone, address).](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=648.0)
  - [**Target Criteria:**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
      - [**Location:** Exmoor National Park region, including a \~20km radius.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1396.0)
      - [**Priority Area:** Linton & Lynmouth.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1415.0)
      - [**Property Type:** Entire properties with a kitchen (not single rooms).](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [**Deliverable:** A spreadsheet of 250–500+ new prospects.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1566.0)
  - [**Integration:** Upload data to Freshdesk CRM to validate against existing contacts and personalize outreach.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1308.0)

### Payment & Project Structure

  - [**Phase 1: Data Collection**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1245.0)
      - [**Scope:** Scrape data and deliver a spreadsheet.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1250.0)
      - [**Cost:** Fixed fee of \~$300–$400, paid upfront.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1245.0)
  - [**Phase 2: Outreach & Conversion**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1125.0)
      - [**Scope:** Execute a multi-channel outreach campaign (email, phone, mail).](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=262.0)
      - [**Cost:** Commission-based, paid on the first booking of a new property.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

### Context: Business Strategy

  - [**"Best of Exmoor":** A local agency for Exmoor National Park properties.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)
  - [**"BookingBrain":** A scalable property management software for agencies worldwide.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [**Rationale:** Focus on growing "Best of Exmoor" inventory to fund "BookingBrain" development.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Leonid:**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1580.0)
      - [Research scraping feasibility and refine cost estimate.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1245.0)
      - [Send a formal proposal and invoice to Chris via email.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1620.0)
  - [**Chris:**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1620.0)
      - [Pay the invoice upon receipt.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1620.0)
  - [**Communication:**](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1588.0)
      - [Use email (`stay@thebestofexmoor.co.uk`) for all project communication.](https://fathom.video/share/3BWGcN9x7oy6iVmMyQW7PcEN7c1LphcR?tab=summary&timestamp=1588.0)

action_items.description: Research Exmoor/Lynton-Lynmouth owner data sources; send recap to Chris (email)
Email Chris invoice w/ milestones (~$300-400) for Exmoor/Lynton-Lynmouth owner data; start scraping on payment
